sources.name: Semantic Scholar API
sources.query: Primary engine for S01-S16, S19-S22. Supports field-of-study filtering, citation graph traversal, and bulk DOI resolution.

sources.name: Google Scholar
sources.query: Secondary engine for all academic searches. Better coverage of economics journals (Econometrica, AER, JET) and older working papers not indexed by S2.

sources.name: arXiv preprint server
sources.query: cs.GT, cs.MA, cs.LG, cs.AI categories. Primary source for MARL papers (S13-S16) and recent game theory preprints (S04, S11, S21).

sources.name: JSTOR
sources.query: Historical auction theory and mechanism design papers (S02, S05). Covers Econometrica, Journal of Economic Theory, Games and Economic Behavior back catalogs.

sources.name: Morgan, Steiglitz, Reis 2003 — Spite bidding in common-value auctions
sources.query: Seed for S02. Foundational spite-bidding model — closest existing theory to Zoo's dumping mechanic. Forward-cite for post-2003 spite/sabotage auction literature.

sources.name: Kamenica & Gentzkow 2011 — Bayesian Persuasion
sources.query: Seed for S07. Information design framework — Zoo's involuntary signaling through placement is a persuasion-adjacent problem. Forward-cite for multi-receiver extensions.

sources.name: Bergemann & Morris 2019 — Information Design: A Unified Perspective
sources.query: Seed for S07. Survey of information design post-Bayesian persuasion. Covers multi-agent settings relevant to Zoo's 9-player type-inference problem.

sources.name: Yu et al. 2022 — The Surprising Effectiveness of PPO in Cooperative Multi-Agent Games
sources.query: Seed for S13. MAPPO reference paper. Forward-cite for competitive/mixed-motive extensions and scalability results beyond cooperative settings.

sources.name: Jaderberg et al. 2017 — Population Based Training of Neural Networks
sources.query: Seed for S15. PBT methodology for hyperparameter search in multi-agent training. Forward-cite for PBT applications in game-playing agents.

sources.name: Lanctot et al. 2019 — OpenSpiel
sources.query: Seed for S15. Framework reference for multi-agent game environments and evaluation metrics. Check if Zoo-like games exist in OpenSpiel's game catalog.

sources.name: Devil's Plan Fandom Wiki
sources.query: S17. Primary rule source — episode-by-episode game breakdowns, player conditions listed, tile mechanics documented. Cross-reference with episode footage for ambiguities.

sources.name: Reddit r/TheDevilsPlan
sources.query: S17, S18. Fan discussion threads dissecting Zoo mechanics, strategy debates, and rule clarifications. Search: 'Zoo rules', 'Zoo strategy', 'Zoo game mechanics'.

sources.name: YouTube — Devil's Plan analysis channels
sources.query: S17, S18. Search: 'Devil's Plan Zoo game analysis', 'Devil's Plan game theory'. Video breakdowns often capture rule details not in text sources.

sources.name: Cooper & Fang 2008 — Understanding Overbidding in Second Price Auctions
sources.query: Seed for S02. Behavioral overbidding parallels strategic dumping — agents paying more than value to impose costs. Check if spite motive is isolated from joy-of-winning motive.

sources.name: SSRN Economics Research Network
sources.query: Supplementary source for S02, S05, S08. Working papers on auction theory and mechanism design not yet published in journals.

sources.name: PettingZoo multi-agent RL library
sources.query: S13, S14. Environment API reference for implementing Zoo as a PettingZoo-compatible environment. Check existing environments for structural parallels.

sources.name: Rosenthal 1973 — A class of games possessing pure-strategy Nash equilibria
sources.query: Seed for S03. Foundational congestion games paper. Zoo's dumping congestion effect may fit within or extend this framework. Check Milchtaich (1996) for weighted variants.

sources.name: Netflix Devil's Plan S1 episodes
sources.query: S17. Authoritative rule source — watch Zoo episode(s) for exact tile placement mechanics, round structure, scoring, tie-breaking, and condition reveal timing.

sources.name: ACM Digital Library
sources.query: Supplementary for S04, S13-S16. Conference proceedings (AAMAS, NeurIPS, ICML) for multi-agent RL and computational game theory papers.

sources.name: Connected Papers
sources.query: Verification phase. Visual citation graph exploration starting from seed papers to identify clusters we may have missed with keyword search alone.
